response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned generalist engineer with ~15 years of experience and recent, relevant exposure to MCP servers and agentic-workflow tooling, but he falls well short of the core requirement of 8+ years building production AI solutions — his GenAI experience spans roughly one year. There is no demonstrated experience with RAG architectures, vector databases, AI data pipelines, evaluation/observability frameworks, or Python, all of which are central to this staff-level forward-deployed role. His strong TypeScript, cloud, and customer-facing teaching background partially offset this, but the AI depth gap is too large for a staff GenAI position.
strengths: Hands-on MCP server and agentic-workflow infrastructure experience at current role
15+ years of broad production engineering (TypeScript, Node.js, AWS, high-throughput systems)
Proven communicator: workshops, conference talks, and mentoring align with forward-deployed responsibilities
concerns: ~1 year of GenAI work versus the required 8+ years building AI-driven solutions
No evidence of RAG, vector databases, LLM evaluation, or multi-agent frameworks (LangGraph/CrewAI)
Python absent from skills; AI-on-cloud and data-pipeline experience not demonstrated
